formula_brief: (indneutralize(((correlation(delta(close, 1), delta(delay(close, 1), 1), 250) * delta(close, 1)) / close), IndClass.subindustry) / sum(((delta(close, 1) / delay(close, 1))^2), 250))
code:
def compute_factor(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.Series:
    # Sort by (code, date) for per-code rolling operations
    df = df.sort(['code', 'date'])

    # delta(close, 1) -> 1-period change in close
    delta_close = delta(pl.col('close'), periods=1)

    # delay(close, 1) -> 1-period delayed close
    delay_close = delay(pl.col('close'), periods=1)

    # delta(delay(close, 1), 1) -> 1-period change of the delayed close
    delta_delay_close = delta(delay_close, periods=1)

    # 250-day rolling correlation between delta(close, 1) and delta(delay(close, 1), 1)
    corr_val = correlation(delta_close, delta_delay_close, window=250)

    # Numerator: (correlation * delta(close, 1)) / close
    numerator = (corr_val * delta_close) / pl.col('close')

    # Use neutralize (available in namespace) instead of indneutralize
    neutralized = neutralize(numerator).over('date')

    # Denominator: sum(((delta(close, 1) / delay(close, 1))^2), 250)
    ratio = delta_close / delay_close
    ratio_sq = ratio ** 2
    denom = rolling_sum(ratio_sq, window=250)

    # Final factor value
    factor = neutralized / denom

    return df.select(factor).to_series()
